heading: Overhaul of naval vessels: competition between public and private shipyards
label: 8684 Overhaul of naval vessels: competition between public and private shipyards
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 8684 was renumbered .section 9252 of this title
Amendments2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 7314 of this title1989— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 101–189section 7313 of this title

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019

Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–456, div. A, title XII, § 1225(b)102 Stat. 2055Sept. 29, 1988“Section 7313 [now 8684] of title 10, United States Code, as added by subsection (a), applies to any award by the Secretary of the Navy made after the end of the 30-day period beginning on the date of the enactment of this Act [] for repair, alteration, overhaul, or conversion of a naval vessel following competition between public and private shipyards.”Sept. 29, 1988

Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–456, div. A, title XII, § 1225(b)102 Stat. 2055Sept. 29, 1988“Section 7313 [now 8684] of title 10, United States Code, as added by subsection (a), applies to any award by the Secretary of the Navy made after the end of the 30-day period beginning on the date of the enactment of this Act [] for repair, alteration, overhaul, or conversion of a naval vessel following competition between public and private shipyards.”Sept. 29, 1988

children.label: A
children.text: the full costs to the United States associated with future retirement benefits of civilian employees of that shipyard consistent with computation methodology established by Office of Management and Budget Circular A–76; and

children.label: B
children.text: in a case in which equal access to the Navy supply system is not allowed to public and private shipyards, a pro rata share of the costs of the Navy supply system.

Costs applicable to oversight of the contract by the appropriate Navy supervisor of shipbuilding, conversion, and repair are added to the bid of any private shipyard for the purpose of comparability analysis.
The award is made using the results of the comparability analysis.
